title: Cell Preparation for Electroporation of Aurantiochytrium using Salt and Sucrose solutions
steps: Harvest 2x 1.5 ml of cells from a culture beyond three days maturity. Centrifuge in microcentrifuge tubes at 4 C, 5 min at 12000 g.
Discard supernatant and resuspend pellet in 1 ml of salt solution (10 mM KCl, 10 mM NaCl, and 3 mM CaCl2). Centrifuge at 4 C, 5 min at 12000 g.
Discard supernatant and resuspend pellet in 1 ml of sucrose solution (50 mM sucrose). Centrifuge at 4 C, 5 min at 12000 g.
Discard supernatant and resuspend pellet in 1 ml of salt solution (10 mM KCl, 10 m M NaCl, and 3 mM CaCl2). Centrifuge at 4 C, 5 min at 12000 g.

Discard supernatant and resuspend pellet in an adequate volume of 50 mM sucrose solution. Cell concentration should be around 4x10^7. Keep cells on ice. Completion of protocol results in 15% survivorship.
creator: Mariana Rius
